1: Incline your ears to the words of my mouth.
2: I will utter dark sayings of old:
3: And our fathers have told us.
4: The praises of the LORD LORD , and his strength, and his wonderful works that he hath done.
5: Which he commanded our fathers, that they should make them known to their children:
6: And declare them them to their children:
7: And not forget the works of God, but keep his commandments:
8: A generation that that set not their heart aright, and whose spirit was not stedfast with God.
9: Turned back in the day of battle.
10: And refused to walk in his law;
11: And his wonders that he had shewed them.
12: In In the field of Zoan.
13: And he made the waters to stand as an heap.
14: And all the night with a light of fire.
15: And gave them them drink as out of out of the great depths.
16: And caused waters to run down like rivers.
17: By provoking the most High in the wilderness.
18: By asking meat for their lust.
19: They said, Can God furnish a table in the wilderness?
20: Can he give bread also? Can he provide flesh for his people?
21: So a fire was kindled against Jacob, and anger also came up against Israel;
22: And trusted not in his salvation:
23: And opened the doors of heaven,
24: And had given them of the corn of heaven.
25: He sent them meat to the full.
26: And by his power he brought in the south wind.
27: And feathered fowls like as the sand of the sea:
29: So they did eat, and were well filled: For he gave them their own desire;
30: But while their meat was was yet in their mouths,
31: And smote down the chosen men men of Israel.
32: And believed not for his wondrous works.
34: And they returned and enquired early after God.
35: And the high God their redeemer.
36: And they lied unto him with their tongues.
37: Neither were they stedfast in his covenant.
38: And did not stir up all his wrath.
39: A wind that passeth away, and cometh not again.
40: And And grieve him in the desert!
41: And limited the Holy One of Israel.
42: Nor Nor the day when he delivered them from the enemy.
43: And his wonders in the field of Zoan:
44: And their floods, that they could not drink.
46: And their labour unto the locust.
47: And their sycomore trees with frost.
48: And their flocks to hot thunderbolts.
49: By sending evil angels among them among them .
50: But gave their life over to the pestilence;
51: The chief of their their strength in the tabernacles of Ham:
52: And guided them in the wilderness like a flock.
53: But the sea overwhelmed their enemies.
54: Even to Even to this mountain, which which his right hand had purchased.
55: And divided them an inheritance by line, and made the tribes of Israel to dwell in their tents.
57: They were turned aside like a deceitful bow.
58: And moved him to jealousy with their graven images.
60: The tent which which he placed among men;
61: And his glory into the enemy's hand.
62: And was wroth with his inheritance.
63: And their maidens were not given to marriage.
64: And their widows made no lamentation.
65: And And like a mighty man that shouteth by reason of wine.
66: He put them to a perpetual reproach.
67: And chose not the tribe of Ephraim:
68: The mount Zion which he loved.
69: Like the earth which he hath established for ever.
70: And took him from the sheepfolds:
71: To feed Jacob his people, and Israel his inheritance.
72: And guided them by the skilfulness of his hands.
